title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Data Contract Brief: Declarative Thinking
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql declarative thinking increment in a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Declarative Thinking · Theory Declarative Thinking
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 6 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Declarative Thinking contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Declarative Thinking · Theory Declarative Thinking.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy. For Predict Declarative Thinking · Theory Declarative Thinking, keep Declarative Thinking connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: State the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits. Apply that move specifically to Declarative Thinking while producing a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

content.title: Worked case pattern

Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary. A defensible solution applies Declarative Thinking, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: SQL requires the author to prescribe every loop and access path. This fails the Declarative Thinking contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Declarative Thinking evidence · Theory Declarative Thinking
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-declarative-thinking-1ufri4" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Declarative Thinking evidence · Theory Declarative Thinking.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Declarative Thinking into observable performance evidence. For Build Declarative Thinking evidence · Theory Declarative Thinking, keep Declarative Thinking connected to executable relational evidence.
